reasoning: Vietnamese tones divide into two groups; the sharp (trắc) group comprises sắc, hỏi, ngã, nặng.
The flat (bằng) group comprises ngang (no mark) and huyền (grave).
In the 6-syllable line, syllable 2 must be flat; từ (huyền) is flat, ở (hỏi) is sharp.
Syllable 3 is unconstrained for tone class in lục bát, so either quán (sắc) or rạp (nặng) fits.
Syllable 4 must be sharp; khách (sắc) is sharp, tươi (ngang) is flat.
The 6th syllable of line 1 just has to rhyme with the 6th of line 2; either la (flat) or khác (sharp) can work depending on the choice in the next line.
To match the rhyme on syllable 6, either đã (rhymes with la) or ách (rhymes with khác) can be used; both satisfy the meter when paired appropriately.
